_comment: ML-review backlog — the parking lot for AI/ML EXPERIMENT follow-ups: new manifests to try, new features to engineer, new dataset families to build, new target horizons / model families to evaluate, promotion-criteria notes (what a model would need to meet next time to clear the shadow->advisory gate). The autonomous /ml-review skill drains this list every run: it triages each open item, converts the resolved ones into experiments_proposed[] or promotion_recommendations[], marks them, or leaves them open with new evidence appended. Do NOT use this for system bugs (those go to health-review-backlog.json) or strategy trading follow-ups (those go to performance-review-backlog.json). Item ids: MB-YYYYMMDD-NNN.

items.source: /ml-review 2026-05-27 — first ml-review session post 2026-05-26 three-way split.
items.title: Add yfinance/ES=F intraday adapter to scripts/ops/build_trainer_datasets.sh so MES regime + setup-quality manifests stop failing every daily cycle
items.description: Three MES manifests fail every trainer cycle with FileNotFoundError at datasets-out/{market_features/MES/{5m,15m},setup_labels/MES/all}/v002/data.jsonl (mes-regime-5m/15m, mes-setup-quality). Root cause already diagnosed in BL-20260526-002 (health-review backlog) — build_trainer_datasets.sh only builds BTCUSDT via the Bybit V5 offvm adapter; the yfinance/ES=F adapter lives in the separate scripts/ops/run_mes_training.sh one-shot but isn't wired into the daily cycle. Net effect: ict-trainer.service exits with rc=1 every cycle even though all 12 BTC manifests succeed. Fix is a build-script extension (Tier-1 trainer-VM tooling, autonomous) — mirror the build_btcusdt_pair helper from PR #2062 for ES=F at 5m + 15m, plus a build_family setup_labels --symbol-scope MES at v002 invocation. Empty dataset is fine (gets manifest_skipped via empty_dataset instead of manifest_failed). This is the ml-review-backlog mirror of BL-20260526-002 — that one tracks the system-health symptom (trainer.service rc=1); this one tracks the ml-pipeline experiment (get the MES models actually training).
items.trigger_condition: Any /ml-review run; or any session attempting to train MES models.
items.resolution_criteria: Next trainer cycle log shows mes-regime-{5m,15m} + mes-setup-quality manifests either manifest_ok (real shards built) or manifest_skipped via empty_dataset (no data yet). cycle_end overall_rc=0. Distinct from BL-20260525-004 which is a separate ml-modeling limitation (mes-regime models collapse to majority class even when shards exist).
evidence_log: 2026-05-28 (/ml-review, cycle run 20260528T000520Z): re-confirmed unfixed. mes-regime-15m, mes-regime-5m, mes-setup-quality => manifest_failed (FileNotFoundError on MES market_features/setup_labels datasets); mes-execution-quality + mes-trade-outcome-winrate => manifest_skipped(empty_dataset); all 12 BTC/global manifests manifest_ok; cycle_end overall_rc=1; ict-trainer.service is-active=failed, ExecMainStatus=1. Root cause: MES datasets existed only at the stale v001 (built by a 2026-05-22 run_serious_baseline.sh one-shot); manifests reference v002. datasets-out build_end overall_rc=0 but only BTCUSDT market_features (215838 rows) built — no MES families. Build-script wiring missing.

2026-05-28 (model-training, PR #2226): FIX SHIPPED + VALIDATED on the trainer (branch claude/zen-gauss-7E7Re). Added build_mes_market() to build_trainer_datasets.sh (yfinance ES=F 5m base -> resample 15m -> median-calibrated v002 market_features; symbol-scoped MES setup_labels). Validation run BUILD_EXIT=0: market_features/MES/5m=10852 rows (vol_threshold 0.0002637), 15m=3617 (0.0004862), setup_labels/MES/all=0 (empty -> clean skip). Pending: merge to main + one real daily cycle showing manifest_ok/skipped + cycle_end rc=0 to fully close.

2026-05-28 (model-training): RESOLVED. #2226 merged to main (sha 71879f0); kicked a real cycle (head=71879f0, manifest_count=20). All MES manifests now clean: mes-regime-5m + mes-regime-15m => manifest_ok (data built, market_features/MES/5m=10852, 15m=3617); mes-setup-quality + mes-execution-quality + mes-trade-outcome-winrate => manifest_skipped(empty_dataset). cycle_end overall_rc=0 (was rc=1 every cycle). ExecMainStatus=0.

items.source: /ml-review 2026-05-27 — re-verification of BL-20260525-004 (MES regime models collapse to one class).
items.title: Experiment: forward-vol-aware vol feature for MES regime classifiers — backward vol_bucket doesn't separate forward-vol regime label on MES
items.description: After today's cycle: mes-regime-5m macro_f1=0.345, mes-regime-15m macro_f1=0.329 (both flat across 11 runs). The RegimeClassifierTrainer (ml/trainers/regime_classifier.py) is a per-bucket modal-class classifier keyed on the BACKWARD-LOOKING vol_bucket; on MES that backward vol_bucket does not separate the (forward-vol-based) regime_label, so every bucket is range-majority and argmax always picks 'range' (f1_volatile=0). BTC works because BTC vol is autocorrelated; MES intraday isn't. Three concrete experiment paths to try, in order of cheapness: (1) more vol buckets (5+ instead of 2-3) so the trainer can carve out sub-regimes that DO separate forward vol; (2) probability-threshold eval instead of argmax — surface the model's volatile-class probability and tune a threshold over the eval split; (3) a stronger continuous vol feature (e.g. realized-vol-of-realized-vol, or a forward-vol-AWARE feature like the prior bar's range expansion rate). Operator decision 2026-05-25: leave manifests as-is until a dedicated model-training session — this is that session's queue.
items.trigger_condition: Next /ml-review run; or whenever a model-training session is scheduled with capacity for MES experiments.
items.resolution_criteria: mes-regime-{5m,15m} eval shows f1_volatile > 0 with macro_f1 > 0.50 via one of the three approaches; or the operator approves a manifest deprecation. Distinct from MB-20260527-001 (which is about MES *trade-data* manifests, not regime; those are empty_dataset blocked until MES trades).
items.status_history: open -> resolved 2026-05-28 (mes-regime-{5m,15m}-lgbm-v2 shipped in #2226; f1_volatile 0->0.77/0.76, macro_f1 0.69/0.67 on median-calibrated data; registered at shadow in cycle head=71879f0). The three vol-bucket tweaks are moot — the LightGBM port solved the collapse.
evidence_log: 2026-05-28 (/ml-review): MES regime manifests failed again (no fresh MES dataset; blocked on MB-20260527-001). Last successful train 2026-05-26: mes-regime-15m macro_f1=0.329, mes-regime-5m macro_f1=0.345, both f1_volatile=0 (still collapsed to range-majority). NEW RECOMMENDATION: the three vol-bucket tweaks are now lower-priority than porting the winning v2 LightGBM recipe to MES. On BTC the LightGBM head lifted f1_volatile from 0 (modal baseline) to 0.228 (15m) / 0.111 (5m) on the same market_features schema with continuous vol + log-return features and inverse-base-rate class weighting (class_weight volatile:50). Propose mes-regime-{5m,15m}-lgbm-v2 manifests mirroring btc-regime-*-lgbm-v2 once MES data is wired (MB-20260527-001). This is the proven fix for the exact collapse symptom.

2026-05-28 (model-training, PR #2226): RESOLVED pending merge. Created mes-regime-{5m,15m}-lgbm-v2 (+ btc-regime-1h-lgbm-v2) and validated on the trainer against freshly-built median-calibrated MES data. f1_volatile went from 0 (collapsed modal baseline) to: mes-regime-5m-lgbm-v2 macro_f1=0.694 / f1_volatile=0.770 (n_eval=2170); mes-regime-15m-lgbm-v2 macro_f1=0.666 / f1_volatile=0.761 (n_eval=723); btc-regime-1h-lgbm-v2 macro_f1=0.637 / f1_volatile=0.490 (n_eval=4210). Collapse solved. MES eval sets are small (20% holdout of the ~60d yfinance window) — deeper IBKR history (MB-20260528-002) needed before any shadow->advisory promotion. Once merged the models register at shadow on the next cycle.

items.title: Decision: setup-quality-lgbm-v2 (mae 0.071 vs baseline mae 0.048 at n=76) — hold for n>=1000 or demote
items.description: setup-quality-lgbm-v2 trained cleanly (PR #2053's LightGBM stack works end-to-end) but loses to setup-quality-baseline-v0 on the same n=76 eval: MAE 0.0710 vs 0.0478, MSE 0.0114 vs 0.0092. Cause is dataset size, not model quality — 76 closed trades is too small for a gradient-boosted tree to beat a simple per-group-mean baseline. The model is currently target_deployment_stage=shadow + auto-wired into the shadow factory (default-flip 2026-05-19), so it logs predictions on every live signal. That's fine for data collection but means it shouldn't be considered for shadow->advisory promotion until either (a) on a fresh cycle with n_eval >= ~1000 it beats baseline on MAE+MSE, OR (b) it's explicitly demoted to research_only via `python -m ml promote-stage setup-quality-lgbm-v2 --new-stage research_only` (semantic change to the registry, operator-approved). This is the ml-review-backlog mirror of BL-20260526-003 (health-review backlog) — same model, recorded here so /ml-review picks it up on each run.
items.trigger_condition: Each /ml-review run; revisit specifically when n_trades >= ~1000 closed trades suitable for setup_labels, OR a dedicated model-training session reviews the v2 setup-quality manifest.
items.resolution_criteria: Either (a) on a fresh training cycle with n_eval >= 1000 the v2 model beats the baseline on MAE+MSE, in which case promote toward advisory; OR (b) operator approves demotion to research_only (which would also stop the shadow predictions); OR (c) manifest is retired (removed from ml/configs/).
evidence_log: 2026-05-28 (/ml-review, run 20260528T000544Z): re-verified, still loses. setup-quality-lgbm-v2 mae=0.0701 mse=0.0112 vs setup-quality-baseline-v0 mae=0.0464 mse=0.0089 at n_eval=78 (was 76 on 5/27 — only +2 closed trades in a week). Dataset still ~13x below the ~1000 threshold needed for the GBT to beat the per-group-mean baseline. Hold; revisit when setup_labels n_eval crosses ~1000. Shadow predictions confirmed flowing (n=14 in 48-min tail, score range -0.007..0.007 — responsive, unlike the constant baselines).

2026-05-29 (/ml-review, cycle run 20260529T002940Z head=b149b15): unchanged — still loses. setup-quality-lgbm-v2 mae=0.07064 mse=0.01125 vs setup-quality-baseline-v0 mae=0.04619 mse=0.00892, both at n_eval=78 (zero new closed trades since 5/28). Shadow predictions confirmed flowing and responsive: count=438 since 2026-05-28, score_mean=-0.0031, range -0.0098..0.0074 — the only setup-family model that is NOT a constant (the three baselines emit fixed per-group means: setup-quality-baseline-v0 const -0.034, audit-baseline-v0 const -0.057). Hold; still ~13x below the n>=1000 threshold.

items.source: /ml-review 2026-05-27 — v2 BTC LightGBM regime models tracking toward shadow→advisory promotion.
items.title: Verify v2 BTC LightGBM regime models' soak completion + draft the shadow→advisory promotion packet (target 2026-06-02 16:08Z)
items.description: btc-regime-15m-lgbm-v2 (macro_f1=0.568, f1_volatile=0.227, n=16834) and btc-regime-5m-lgbm-v2 (macro_f1=0.546) both beat their baselines (0.491 / 0.499 respectively — baselines collapse to majority with f1_volatile=0). After the 2026-05-26 retraining cycle they entered the current shadow stage at 16:08:23Z (15m) and 16:08:40Z (5m) per BL-20260526-001 — 7-day soak targets ~2026-06-02 16:08Z. Engineering work is fully landed; only the time-based promotion gates remain (shadow_soak >= 7 days; beats_baseline / live_agreement / drift_clean from accumulating live shadow predictions). When the soak completes, this skill should: (1) pull shadow_predictions.jsonl + verify entries exist for both model_ids tied to closed live BTCUSDT trades; (2) compute brier_lift, AUC, and the shadow-drift report so beats_baseline/live_agreement/drift_clean clear; (3) emit a promotion_recommendations[] entry with evidence; the operator decides shadow→advisory. Mirror of BL-20260526-001 (health-review backlog) — kept here too so /ml-review naturally picks it up on the 6/2 cadence.
items.trigger_condition: Any /ml-review run on or after 2026-06-02; or any session investigating 'are the v2 BTC models ready to promote shadow->advisory?'.
items.resolution_criteria: Verified: (1) shadow_predictions.jsonl carries entries for btc-regime-{5m,15m}-lgbm-v2 tied to closed live BTCUSDT trades; (2) brier_lift, AUC>0.55, drift report present so beats_baseline/live_agreement/drift_clean clear; (3) evidence packet prepared and an explicit promotion_recommendations[] entry emitted. Operator-gated decision; this skill prepares the packet.
evidence_log: 2026-05-28 (/ml-review) progress check (soak still running, target ~6/2 16:08Z): (a) shadow predictions CONFIRMED flowing — btc-regime-5m-lgbm-v2 emitting in the live shadow log (n=10 in a 48-min tail window, score mean 0.918 = volatile-class prob); btc-regime-5m-baseline-v1 alongside it (mean 0.988). 15m variants fire on 15m cadence so absent from the short tail but are wired. (b) Training edge holds after today's retrain: btc-regime-15m-lgbm-v2 macro_f1=0.568 / f1_volatile=0.228 vs baseline 0.491 / 0.000; btc-regime-5m-lgbm-v2 macro_f1=0.547 / f1_volatile=0.111 vs baseline 0.499 / 0.000 (n_eval 16853 / 50568). (c) Realized join NOT yet computable — only 3 closed BTCUSDT trades in the pulled window (all small losses) and the predictions-to-closed-trades join needs /api/bot/trades/scores. Packet still pending soak completion; no promotion recommended this run.

2026-05-29 (/ml-review) progress check (soak target ~6/2 16:08Z, ~4 days out): (a) Training edge HOLDS, flat run-over-run (cycle b149b15): btc-regime-15m-lgbm-v2 macro_f1=0.5676 / f1_volatile=0.2259 vs baseline-v1 0.4909 / 0.000 (n=16872); btc-regime-5m-lgbm-v2 macro_f1=0.5455 / f1_volatile=0.1066 vs baseline-v1 0.4989 / 0.000 (n=50626). btc-regime-1h-lgbm-v2 is the strongest BTC regime: macro_f1=0.6327 / f1_volatile=0.4830 (n=4214). (b) **SHADOW-FEED ANOMALY — soak evidence at risk:** in shadow_stats since 2026-05-28 the btc-regime-5m {baseline,lgbm} models last_seen=2026-05-28T22:29:34Z (count=362) — they STOPPED emitting ~15.7h before the 14:10Z pull, while all 7 trade-family shadow models kept emitting through 2026-05-29T14:10Z (count=438). The 15m + 1h v2 regime variants emit ZERO shadow predictions at all (not in the shadow log) — likely not wired into the live shadow factory, or a regime-feed stall. This compromises the 6/2 promotion soak: the order-influencing evidence the gate needs is not continuously accumulating. Flagged for /health-review (live-VM pipeline plumbing). (c) Realized join still NOT computable (no /api/bot/trades/scores diag mirror; only shadow_stats aggregates reachable from a web session). No promotion recommended this run; the soak should not be considered clean until the regime shadow feed is confirmed continuous.

items.source: /ml-review 2026-05-28 — registry sweep found an orphaned MES daily-regime model.
items.title: Orphan model mes-regime-classifier-baseline-v0 (MES/1d) — never retrains, no active manifest; wire a recurring 1d manifest or deprecate
items.description: mes-regime-classifier-baseline-v0 sits at stage=shadow with a single history entry ('initial registration', run 2026-05-22T09:05:39Z, code_rev c0b14ff1) and has not retrained in 6 days. It is NOT in the daily cycle's 17-manifest set (those are mes-regime-5m / mes-regime-15m at 5m/15m, plus the BTC/global set) — so it is an orphan left over from an earlier experiment. Notably its frozen metrics are the BEST of any MES regime model: macro_f1=0.685, accuracy=0.748, f1_volatile=0.543, n_eval=1292 on the MES/1d market_features dataset — i.e. on the DAILY timeframe the regime label IS separable (unlike the 5m/15m models that collapse to f1_volatile=0, MB-20260527-002). Two clean resolutions: (a) if the 1d MES regime signal is worth keeping, add an ml/configs/mes-regime-1d.yaml to the daily cycle so it retrains on fresh data and stays current; (b) if it was a one-off probe, deprecate it (promote-stage research_only or remove) so it stops occupying a shadow slot and auto-wiring onto strategies via the default-flip. Either way it should not linger as a stale shadow model.
items.trigger_condition: Next /ml-review run; or any model-training session touching MES manifests.
items.resolution_criteria: Either (a) ml/configs/mes-regime-1d.yaml exists and mes-regime-classifier-baseline-v0 (or its successor) shows fresh retrains in the cycle log; OR (b) operator approves deprecation (promote-stage research_only / manifest removal) and the model no longer sits at shadow.
evidence_log: 2026-05-29 (/ml-review, cycle b149b15): still orphaned, still not retraining. mes-regime-classifier-baseline-v0 stage=research_only (NOTE: now research_only, not shadow as the 5/28 entry recorded — it does not occupy a shadow slot, lowering urgency), last train still 2026-05-22T09:05:39Z (run 20260522T090539Z, code_rev c0b14ff1), 7 days stale, NOT in the 20-manifest daily set. Its frozen metrics remain the best of any MES regime model: macro_f1=0.6845, accuracy=0.7477, f1_volatile=0.5434, n_eval=1292 on MES/1d v001 market_features. The 1d timeframe separates the regime label where 5m/15m collapse the modal baseline. Now that native IBKR MES history is wired (MB-20260528-002 resolved), a recurring mes-regime-1d.yaml on v002 would let this signal retrain on fresh deep data. Converted to experiments_proposed[] (new_manifest: mes-regime-1d) this run; leaving open until the manifest lands or the operator deprecates.

items.source: Operator asked why MES isn't getting better (IBKR) data during the 2026-05-28 model-training session.
items.title: Deeper MES history: run the IBKR historical pull ON the live VM (shares the one IB gateway) and sync to trainer; resolve the Error 162 session conflict
items.description: MES regime models currently train on a rolling ~60-day ES=F 5m window from yfinance — the deepest intraday history yfinance serves and the only source reachable from the trainer VM. IBKR would give multi-year 1m MES history, but the architecture blocks it: (1) IB Gateway is a loopback-only socket on the LIVE VM (127.0.0.1:4002 via the gnzsnz socat relay) — the trainer VM cannot reach it. (2) IBKR permits exactly ONE logged-in session per username, so we can't run a second gateway on the trainer without disconnecting the live MES trader. The ibkr_offvm adapter (ml/datasets/adapters/ibkr_offvm.py) is therefore designed to run ON the live VM (guard ICT_IB_HISTORICAL_OK=1), sharing the live gateway on a DISTINCT clientId, paced gently (~12s between chunked reqHistoricalData calls), then the resulting market_raw shards sync to the trainer. (3) Current blocker: run_serious_baseline.sh's MES leg is gated behind MES_IBKR=1 (default 0) and was disabled 2026-05-22 because the gateway returned IBKR Error 162 'session connected from a different IP'. The 2026-05-28 IB-gateway auto-heal watchdog (ict-ib-gateway-watchdog) mitigates the overnight reset-wedge but does not address the historical-pull session conflict. PROPOSED PATH (Tier-2/3, live-VM, operator-gated): add a paced, low-priority IBKR historical-pull job on the LIVE VM (distinct clientId e.g. 450, useRTH=false, pause_s>=12) that writes market_raw/MES/{5m,15m,1m}/v002 and is picked up by the trainer sync; verify it does not perturb the live trading clientId; resolve why Error 162 fires (likely clientId collision or the gateway only accepting one source IP). Until then the ~60d yfinance window is the working fallback and the MES LightGBM models train on it.
items.trigger_condition: Operator wants deeper/cleaner MES training history; or when the IB gateway session conflict (Error 162) is investigated.
items.resolution_criteria: market_raw/MES/{5m,15m} at v002 carries multi-year (or at least multi-month) IBKR-sourced bars synced to the trainer, built without disconnecting or degrading the live MES trading session; mes-regime models retrain on it. OR operator decides the ~60d yfinance window is sufficient and this is closed as won't-do.
evidence_log: 2026-05-28 (model-training): root-caused + mechanism drafted. Live IB gateway is HEALTHY (watchdog probes report healthy=True net_liquidation=1000600.6 every 5 min) — the 2026-05-22 Error 162 was NOT a gateway fault; it came from attempting the pull from the TRAINER (different IP, loopback gateway unreachable / IBKR source-IP guard). Fix = run the pull ON the live VM sharing the one session on a distinct clientId. Drafted (operator-gated, NOT executed): scripts/ops/pull_mes_ibkr_history.sh (live-VM paced ibkr_offvm pull, clientId 450 vs exec 497, pause_s=15, use_rth=false, ~365d MES 5m+15m -> $DATA_DIR/ibkr_datasets); sync_trainer_data.sh extended to rsync those shards to the trainer; build_mes_market prefers the synced IBKR base over yfinance when >1000 rows present. REMAINING (operator): deploy to live VM via pull-and-deploy, then run the script in the CME maintenance/weekend window (shares the gateway pacing budget with live MES candle reqs); verify it does not perturb live trading; confirm the trainer picks up the deeper history on the next cycle.

2026-05-28 (model-training): RESOLVED end-to-end. Operator approved a now-run. pull-mes-ibkr-history system-action added (#2231) + fixed for the live VM's system python / canonical log path / flock single-instance (#2238). Ran on the live VM: clientId 450, paced 20s, system python, live trader heartbeat stayed fresh throughout (zero disruption). Pulled native MES via IBKR: market_raw/MES/5m=16197 rows, 15m=5383, to /data/bot-data/ibkr_datasets. Kicked a cycle: build_mes_market logged 'using synced IBKR MES market_raw (deep history) instead of yfinance' and built MES features from it (5m=16173, 15m=5359, median vol_threshold 5m=0.00056/15m=0.00093); cycle_end rc=0. MES regime models retrained on NATIVE IBKR data: mes-regime-5m-lgbm-v2 macro_f1=0.621 / f1_volatile=0.547 (n=3235); mes-regime-15m-lgbm-v2 macro_f1=0.520 / f1_volatile=0.517 (n=1072) — lower than the earlier ES=F-proxy numbers but HONEST on real data, and still well above the collapsed modal baselines (f1_volatile=0). Depth caveat: ~16k 5m rows (~6mo) for a 1y request — IBKR pages contracts newest-first capped at max_contracts=4; PR #2247 adds MES_MAX_CONTRACTS=8 to page the older 2025 expiries (depth still ultimately capped by IBKR per-contract intraday retention). Item closed; further depth tuning tracked on #2247.

items.source: /ml-review 2026-05-29 — shadow_stats sweep found the higher-timeframe v2 regime models emit zero shadow predictions while only the 5m regime models log (and those stopped at 22:29Z).
items.title: Regime-model shadow coverage: btc/mes-regime-{15m,1h}-lgbm-v2 emit ZERO shadow predictions — wire them into the live shadow factory so they accumulate a track record before any promotion
items.description: shadow_stats since 2026-05-28 returns only 9 model_ids: the 7 trade/setup/exec/prop families (signal-driven, count=438) plus btc-regime-5m-{baseline-v1,lgbm-v2} (count=362). The 15m + 1h v2 regime variants (btc-regime-15m-lgbm-v2, btc-regime-1h-lgbm-v2, mes-regime-{5m,15m}-lgbm-v2) emit NOTHING into shadow_predictions.jsonl — so the strongest BTC regime model (btc-regime-1h-lgbm-v2, macro_f1=0.6327 / f1_volatile=0.4830) and the now-honest MES regime models have no order-influencing track record at all. Separately, the 5m regime models last_seen=2026-05-28T22:29:34Z (stopped ~15.7h before the 14:10Z pull while every signal-driven model continued) — a possible regime-feed stall (that half is /health-review's plumbing domain; logged there too via the recommended_action). ML-coverage gap to own here: confirm which regime model_ids the live shadow factory actually invokes (the default-flip auto-wires shadow models when strategy YAML omits shadow_model_ids, but the regime predictors fire on a market-bar cadence — verify the 15m/1h predictors are actually being called) and extend coverage so all shadow-stage regime models log predictions. Without continuous shadow logging on the higher timeframes, the 6/2 shadow->advisory soak (MB-20260527-004) can only ever evidence the 5m variant.
items.trigger_condition: Next /ml-review run; or any session investigating why a shadow-stage regime model has zero predictions.
items.resolution_criteria: shadow_stats shows non-zero, continuously-updating counts for btc-regime-{15m,1h}-lgbm-v2 and mes-regime-{5m,15m}-lgbm-v2 (last_seen tracking now), so each shadow-stage regime model accumulates an order-influencing track record; OR it is confirmed by-design that only the 5m regime feeds the shadow factory and the higher timeframes are documented as observe-via-training-metrics-only.
